titleStmt.title: No. 88The Secretary of State to the Secretary of Defense (Lovett)

div.head:
No. 88
The Secretary of State to the Secretary of Defense (Lovett)
div.p: My Dear Mr. Secretary: Attached is a memorandumNot printed. For substantially the same text, see telegram 2155 to Paris, Document 94. setting forth proposed United States comments on certain proposals concerning the Middle East Defense Organization put forward by the French Government in a memorandum handed to the Department of State August 15, 1952 (copy attached).Not attached to source text. See the editorial note, supra , and the memorandum of conversation by Daspit, Document 84. A copy of this memorandum was transmitted informally to the Department of Defense at the time of its receipt.For the Department of Defense response, see footnote 2, Document 94.

The French memorandum is addressed principally to the question of the higher military direction of MEDO and emphasizes the importance the French attach to making the NATO Standing Group responsible for the guidance and direction of MEDO. In view of the interest of the Department of Defense in this matter, I would appreciate your advising me as to whether you consider the proposed
memorandum to the French an appropriate expression of United States views.

closer.signed:
For the Secretary of State:
Henry A. Byroade
Assistant Secretary
